article_title: Budget session from January 28; Union Budget on February 1 - The Hindu
article_summary: The Budget session of Parliament is likely to commence on January 28 and the Union Budget is set to be tabled on February 1, a Sunday. Officials, citing the tentative schedule finalised by the Cabinet Committee on Parliamentary Affairs, said on Friday (January 9, 2026) that President Droupadi Murmu will address a joint sitting of the two Houses on January 28. The customary address of the President takes place on the first day of the first Parliament session of the year. The two Houses will not meet on January 29 due to the Beating Retreat ceremony. Parliament will sit on January 30 when the Economic Survey is likely to be tabled. On January 31, the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha will not meet. article_summary: Ahead of the Union Budget, Chief Minister Siddaramaiah has urged the Centre to release its dues and has referred to the economic conditions in the State owing to GST rate rationalization, increased social commitment, change in weather condition, and increasing urbanisation. article_title: When is Union Budget of India? Date, time & key details - The Hindu
article_summary: The Union Budget of India, officially known as the Annual Financial Statement under Article 112 of the Indian Constitution, is the Central government’s financial plan for the upcoming fiscal year (April 1 to March 31). It is a statement of the estimated receipts and expenditure of the government for that particular year. As the Union Budget 2026 approaches, let’s understand some of the key details that one should watch out for this Budget session. What is Union Budget for? The Union Budget is important because it shapes our country's economic strategies and affects families and businesses on a massive scale. article_title: Expectations from a gender lens in Budget 2026-27 - The Hindu
article_summary: As India prepares for Budget 2026-27, it must confront a fundamental, yet persistently ignored, constraint on growth: women’s time poverty. Women contribute only about 18% to India’s GDP, not because they work less, but because a large share of their labour remains unpaid and uncounted in national income. About 40% of India’s women are now in the labour force, largely concentrated in unpaid agricultural work, but 60% of women who are out of the labour force cite domestic and care responsibilities as the reason (Periodic Labour Force Survey data). This is the result of a combination of supply-and-demand side constraints — care burdens, limited skills, mobility barriers, and restrictive social norms and limited creation of decent jobs to absorb women’s time remuneratively. article_summary: **Rohit Pawar**, leader of the NCP (SP), demanded an inquiry into alleged black magic incidents outside the residences of late Maharashtra deputy chief minister **Ajit Pawar**. article_title: Ajit Pawar’s nephew files complaint in Bengaluru against air crash; says Maharashtra police refused to file FIR - The Hindu
article_summary: A **Zero FIR** has been registered in Bengaluru based on a complaint filed by Maharashtra MLA Rohit Rajendra Pawar alleging a criminal conspiracy behind the death of former Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Ajit Pawar in a plane crash. The complaint, filed under Section 173(1) of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, claims systematic violations of aviation safety norms and gross negligence by **VSR Ventures Private Limited**, the aircraft operator. The FIR cites findings from the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA), which found the aircraft to be negligent and not airworthy, leading to its grounding during the investigation. The complaint also raises concerns about the aircraft's history, including its prior use abroad and alleged underreporting of flight hours.

article_title: Zero FIR in Ajit Pawar crash an attempt to defame Maharashtra, says Fadnavis - The Hindu
article_summary: **Maharashtra** Chief Minister **Devendra Fadnavis** stated that the zero FIR registered in Bengaluru regarding the plane crash that killed then Deputy Chief Minister Ajit Pawar was an attempt by the neighboring **Karnataka** government to defame the state. He clarified that a probe is underway by the Criminal Investigation Department and an Accidental Death Report (ADR) has been filed, adding that the state government had also requested a Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) probe as per the family's wishes. **Fadnavis** emphasized that the zero FIR was not intended to hinder an impartial probe and urged **Rohit Pawar**, Ajit Pawar’s nephew, to use legal avenues if he felt aggrieved.

article_title: Former Minister Eshwarappa criticises Chief Minister for his comments, asks him to read Bhagavad Gita - The Hindu
article_summary: Former Minister K.S. Eshwarappa has criticised Chief Minister Siddaramaiah for his comments on introducing Bhagavad Gita into the school curriculum. “The Chief Minister’s statements show his ideological bankruptcy. Let him study the Bhagavad Gita once,” he said in a press conference at Shivamogga on December 8. Mr. Siddaramaiah had called Union Minister H.D. Kumaraswamy a ‘manuwadi’ for demanding the inclusion of the Gita in the school syllabus. Reacting to his statement, Mr. Eshwarappa said, “The Bhagavad Gita is not meant only for Hindus. People belonging to all religions should read it. Can the Chief Minister comment about either the Quran or the Bible in the same way?” he questioned. He thanked Union Minister H.D. article_title: K.S. Eshwarappa comes down heavily on Congress party, State government - The Hindu
article_summary: Former Minister K.S. Eshwarappa has expressed disappointment over the recent developments in the Legislative Assembly and Legislative Council, stating that he has never witnessed the legislators’ use of language stoop so low in his political career. Addressing the media here on February 7, Mr. Eshwarappa, a five-time MLA and former MLC, said that the way K.M. Shivalinge Gowda, Congress MLA, and Naseer Ahmed, Congress MLC, spoke was highly deplorable. “The chairman of the Legislative Council, Basavaraj Horatti, also resorted to using harsh words against legislators, as he could not tolerate the comments made by the Congress MLC,” said Mr. Eshwarappa.
